record.field: Metabase is an open-source business intelligence platform that lets anyone explore data and build dashboards without writing SQL.

Metabase is an open-source business intelligence platform that lets anyone explore data and build dashboards without writing SQL.

It connects to your relational and analytical databases and turns them into self-service analytics: a visual query builder lets non-technical users ask questions, while analysts can drop into a full SQL editor when they need it. Results become charts, dashboards and shareable reports, with an AI assistant (Metabot) to help draft queries and a data modeling layer to define canonical metrics. It is designed for fast, broad adoption across a whole team rather than a small group of specialists.

Key features
  • Visual question builder plus a full SQL editor for power users
  • Interactive dashboards with filters, drill-through and scheduled subscriptions
  • Broad database support (PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, ClickHouse and many more)
  • Granular permissions, groups and data sandboxing for controlled access
  • Embedded analytics to surface charts and dashboards inside other applications
  • AI-assisted querying and a data modeling layer for reusable metrics
Best for

Teams and companies that want self-service analytics and dashboards over their existing databases, giving non-technical users data access while keeping SQL power available for analysts. A strong self-hosted alternative to tools like Looker, Tableau or Power BI.

services: metabase: image: metabase/metabase:v0.50.8 volumes: - /dev/urandom:/dev/random:ro environment: MB_DB_TYPE: postgres MB_DB_DBNAME: metabaseappdb MB_DB_PORT: 5432 MB_DB_USER: metabase MB_DB_PASS: mysecretpassword MB_DB_HOST: postgres healthcheck: test: curl --fail -I http://localhost:3000/api/health || exit 1 interval: 15s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres: image: postgres:14 environment: POSTGRES_USER: metabase POSTGRES_DB: metabaseappdb POSTGRES_PASSWORD: mysecretpassword container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
